originalTitle: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
title: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
content: Ismail Omar Guelleh, 78, has extended his already 27-year rule in a poll boycotted by the main opposition candidates.

title: Nearly 400 sentenced in Nigeria for links to militant Islamists
content: Judges handed down sentences ranging from five years to life imprisonment following mass trials.

originalTitle: UK shelves Chagos deal after Trump opposition
title: UK shelves Chagos deal after Trump opposition
content: UK government officials say they are not entirely abandoning the agreement but have run out of time.

originalTitle: Trump announces Strait of Hormuz blockade after US-Iran peace talks end
title: Trump announces Strait of Hormuz blockade after US-Iran peace talks end
content: US Navy to enforce blockade, Trump says after condemning Iran for blocking waterway, complaining of no deal in Pakistan.

originalTitle: Peru votes for ninth president in less than decade
title: Peru votes for ninth president in less than decade
content: Voters to choose from 35 presidential candidates, including a comedian, a media baron and a political dynasty heiress.

originalTitle: Global Sumud Flotilla sets sail from Barcelona for Gaza
title: Friends Set Sail to Help Gaza!
content:
A Big Journey Starts!

Hello, super friends! Get ready for some exciting news from far, far away. Imagine many big, big boats getting ready to go on a very long trip. These boats are not going on a holiday. Oh no! They are going on a very special mission. This mission is all about helping people. Many, many people stood by the sea. They waved goodbye to these special boats. It was a very important day. Thousands of people watched the boats get ready. They wanted to show their love and support. These boats are carrying lots of good things. Things that can make life better for children and families. It’s like sending a giant hug across the ocean!

Where is Barcelona?

So, where did this big journey start? It started in a beautiful city called Barcelona. Barcelona is in a country named Spain. Spain is very, very far from us here in Africa. It's across a huge ocean! Imagine looking at a big map of the world. You would see Spain way up north, in Europe. And then you would see the long, long path the boats will take across the blue, blue sea. Did you know? Barcelona is famous for its amazing buildings and sunny beaches! It's a place where many people visit and live. But today, it was special because of these boats.

Who Are These Helpers?

Many different people are on these boats. They are kind people from all over the world. They want to help others who are far away. Some are doctors, some are teachers, some are just regular people with big hearts. They packed the boats with important things. They worked hard to get everything ready. They believe that everyone deserves to be happy and safe. These helpers are like superheroes, but instead of capes, they have big boats! They are sailing because they want to share kindness. They want to show that even if you live far apart, you can still care for each other.

What is a Flotilla?

When we say "flotilla," it means a group of boats sailing together. Think of it like a parade of ships! Instead of one boat going alone, many boats go side-by-side. Why do they go together? Because it's safer and stronger. It shows that many people are united. It's like when you and your friends walk to school together. You are a little group, right? Well, a flotilla is a big group of ships! This flotilla is extra special because it's the biggest one yet! Many boats, all going to help. Imagine seeing so many big ships on the water, all heading in the same direction. It must be an amazing sight!

Where Are They Going?

These boats are going to a place called Gaza. Gaza is very far from Barcelona. It is a place that needs a lot of help right now. People there, including many children, are having a tough time. Gaza is a special place by the sea. It has its own history and its own people. But for a while, it has been hard for things to get in and out. That's why these helpful boats are so important. The journey to Gaza will take many days. The boats will sail across big oceans and past many lands. It's a long, long way to go to bring a smile to someone's face.

Why Do People in Gaza Need Help?

Imagine if your town was closed off. It would be hard to get new toys, new clothes, or even some yummy food sometimes. That's a bit like what has been happening in Gaza. It's been hard for important things to get to the people there. Things like medicine for when someone is sick. Things like building blocks to fix homes. Things like school supplies for children to learn. So, these brave people and their big boats are going to try and bring these important things. They want to make sure the children and families in Gaza have what they need to live well. Did you know? Even simple things like pencils and paper can be very important if you don't have enough!

What Are They Carrying?

What kind of treasures are on these boats? Oh, so many! They are not carrying gold or jewels. They are carrying things that are even more precious. They have boxes filled with food. Yummy food like rice, beans, and healthy snacks. They have bottles of clean water. Water is super important for everyone to stay healthy! They also have medicine. If someone gets a little cough or a tummy ache, medicine can help them feel better. And bandages for scraped knees! And guess what else? They might even have some toys! Because every child deserves to play and have fun. They might have school books and crayons too, so children can keep learning.

A Message of Hope

This journey is not just about the things on the boats. It's also about sending a big message. It’s a message that says: "We care about you! You are not forgotten!" When people see these boats coming, they will know that friends from far away are thinking of them. It shows that people around the world can be kind and help each other. It's a message of hope. Hope that things will get better. Hope that children will have what they need. Hope that everyone can live in peace and happiness. So, let's wish these brave boats and their kind helpers a safe journey! May their big hearts bring lots of smiles to Gaza. Did you know? Helping others makes you feel happy too! It's called the "helper's high."
summary: Big boats full of help are sailing from a faraway city called Barcelona to a place called Gaza, where people need kind support.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows how many people around the world care about others and want to make sure everyone has what they need, like food and medicine.

originalTitle: After Iran talks falter, the big question is what happens next?
title: After Iran talks falter, the big question is what happens next?
content: Twenty-one hours was not enough to end 47 years of hostility between Iran and the US, writes the BBC's Lyse Doucet.

originalTitle: Asha Bhosle: The sound of Bollywood dies aged 92
title: Asha Bhosle: The sound of Bollywood dies aged 92
content: Her infectious voice got fans dancing and singing, becoming the soundtrack for generations of Indians.

title: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

originalTitle: Wrexham not down and out yet - Parkinson
title: Wrexham not down and out yet - Parkinson
content: Phil Parkinson says his side are still in the mix to earn a Championship play-off place, despite their blunt showing in a 2-0 loss to Birmingham City.

originalTitle: What is going on with Garnacho?
title: What is going on with Garnacho?
content: Alejandro Garnacho is struggling to stand out at Chelsea - so can the £40m signing from Manchester United still realise his potential at Stamford Bridge?

originalTitle: Union Berlin appoint first female boss in Europe's top men's leagues
title: Union Berlin appoint first female boss in Europe's top men's leagues
content: Marie-Louise Eta becomes the first woman appointed to manage a men's team in one of Europe's top five leagues as she is named interim head coach of Bundesliga side Union Berlin.
